report_name: Ransomware Attacks Surge in Q2 2026, Black Nevas Group Leads Latest Wave

threat_actors.name: Black Nevas
threat_actors.description: The most prolific ransomware group in a recent wave of attacks in Q2 2026, responsible for 9 of 23 new victims in a 24-hour period. The group operates a data leak site as part of a double-extortion strategy and targets various industries including Manufacturing, Real Estate, and Healthcare.

threat_actors.name: CoinbaseCartel
threat_actors.description: An active ransomware group contributing to the surge of attacks in Q2 2026. The group uses a data leak site for double-extortion.

threat_actors.name: Blackwater
threat_actors.description: An active ransomware group contributing to the surge of attacks in Q2 2026. The group uses a data leak site for double-extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups encrypt systems across victim networks to cause business disruption and extort payment.

attack_patterns.name: Data Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive data is exfiltrated from victim networks to attacker-controlled servers before encryption, as part of a double-extortion tactic involving data leak sites.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups delete volume shadow copies using commands like 'wmic.exe shadowcopy delete' to prevent system recovery from snapshots.

identities.name: City of York, Pennsylvania
identities.description: A historical victim of a ransomware attack, mentioned in the report as having paid a $500,000 settlement in 2025 to illustrate the underreporting of such incidents.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Data Leak Sites (DLS)
infrastructure.description: Dedicated sites used by ransomware groups like Black Nevas to publish data from non-paying victims as part of a double-extortion strategy.

indicators.name: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9c5b8f7a-1e3d-4b8c-9a6f-0d7e5c1a2b3d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.purpleops.io/threat-intelligence/ransomware-intelligence-reveals-q2-threats
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
modified: 2026-04-19
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, although rare.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern for deleting volume shadow copies, a behavior associated with ransomware attacks to prevent recovery.
